articles.title: Luka Doncic To Remain Out For Start Of Series Versus Thunder
articles.description: Luka Doncic will remain out for the start of the Los Angeles Lakers' series against the Oklahoma City Thunder, according to Shams Charania of ESPN. Doncic, who is recovering from a Grade 2 hamstring strain, is being evaluated on a week-to-week timeline. "The Lakers are evaluating Doncic on a week-to-week basis. He's missed about a month right now. One source told me in the last few days it's still a slow path on Doncic's recovery. "He's doing more and more on the court, but right now still not full-fledge running or full-contact workouts," said Charania. It was reported on Friday that there is still no timetable for Doncic's return.

articles.title: 📹 Wants a World Cup spot! Endrick scores again, pressures Ancelotti
articles.description: Carlo Ancelotti was at the Maracanã to, for example, watch Pedro in person during this Sunday’s (3) Flamengo vs. Vasco match. And Endrick made a point of stressing that he is still very much alive in the race for a World Cup spot. Lyon fell behind against Rennes after a stunning goal from Jordanian Mousa Tamari just six minutes in. They came from behind even before halftime with Yaremchuk (37') and Tolisso converting a penalty (42'). Lepaul equalized for Rennes right at the start of the second half (48'). The persistent Lyon went back in front thanks to Afonso Moreira (52'). Endrick was already standing out with passes that put his teammates in great positions. But he decided to settle it in the 75th minute, when, “with his weaker foot,” he unleashed a powerful right-footed strike to make it 4-2. The Brazilian striker developed at Palmeiras and owned by Real Madrid reached his eighth goal in 19 matches for the French team. He also has six assists. Lyon reached 60 points in Ligue 1. They are currently in third place and are on course for a spot in the league phase of the next Champions League. Lille (58), Rennes (56), and Monaco (54) are their rivals with three rounds remaining. According to FIFA’s schedule, the CBF must send, on May 11, a preliminary list of 55 players called up for the 2026 World Cup. A week later, on May 18, Carlo Ancelotti will announce the 26 players who will try to win Brazil’s sixth World Cup title at the tournament in the United States, Mexico, and Canada. This article was translated into English by Artificial Intelligence. You can read the original version in 🇧🇷 here.

articles.title: Leading tennis players including Djokovic and Sabalenka unhappy with French Open prize money
articles.description: Group release joint statement over pay concerns Players also call for greater say in tennis schedule A group of the world’s top 20 male and female players, including Novak Djokovic, Jannik Sinner, Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff, have released a joint statement expressing their disappointment at the level of prize money on offer at the French Open later this month. The top players have been in dispute with all four grand slams for more than a year, as they feel they are given an insufficient share of each tournament’s increasing revenues, while they are also calling for enhanced welfare and pension provisions and a greater say in determining the schedule. Continue reading...
